encounter.reason: acute gangrenous perforated appendicitis, status post laparoscopic appendectomy
encounter.location: Nebraska Medicine surgical floor, 4350 Dewey Avenue, Omaha, NE 68105

conditions.name: acute gangrenous perforated appendicitis
conditions.note: Diagnosed clinically at ED encounter; updated with pathological confirmation (transmural necrosis, perforation, acute serositis with fibrinopurulent exudate). Documented as discharge diagnosis status post laparoscopic appendectomy.

conditions.name: periappendiceal abscess
conditions.note: Identified on CT at ED encounter; drained intraoperatively during this admission. Documented as discharge diagnosis.

medications.name: piperacillin-tazobactam 3.375 g intravenous
medications.sig: 3.375 g IV every 6 hours for perioperative infection coverage
medications.reason: perioperative infection coverage for perforated appendicitis and periappendiceal abscess

medications.name: morphine intravenous
medications.sig: IV morphine PRN for post-operative pain

medications.name: acetaminophen intravenous
medications.sig: IV acetaminophen for scheduled pain management

medications.name: amoxicillin-clavulanate 875/125 mg oral tablet
medications.sig: Take one 875/125 mg tablet by mouth twice daily for 7 days; complete course by January 22, 2026
medications.reason: post-operative antibiotic course for perforated appendicitis with abscess

medications.name: acetaminophen 500 mg oral tablet
medications.sig: Take 500 mg by mouth every 6 hours as needed for pain

medications.name: oxycodone 5 mg oral tablet
medications.sig: Take 5 mg by mouth every 4–6 hours as needed for breakthrough pain; dispensed #20 tablets

procedures.name: laparoscopic appendectomy with drainage of periappendiceal abscess
procedures.findings: gangrenous appendix with visible perforation at the tip, ~30 mL purulent fluid in the right paracolic gutter
procedures.details: Abscess cavity irrigated, 10-French Jackson-Pratt drain placed in the right lower quadrant. Operative time 68 minutes. EBL 50 mL. Specimen sent to pathology.

procedures.name: removal of Jackson-Pratt drain
procedures.details: JP drain removed from RLQ; site clean, no leakage

document_references.author: Dr. Priya Nair
document_references.description: Comprehensive discharge summary covering the 3-day inpatient surgical admission for acute gangrenous perforated appendicitis. Documents the hospital course from post-operative arrival through discharge on POD3, including operative summary, daily progress (vitals, diet advancement, drain management, pain control, lab trends), pathology results, discharge diagnoses, medications, wound care instructions, activity restrictions, and follow-up plan.

Reconciliation of inpatient medications to discharge prescriptions, documenting transition from IV piperacillin-tazobactam to oral amoxicillin-clavulanate, and from IV to oral analgesics. Lists three active discharge medications.

Patient-facing discharge instructions covering wound care for laparoscopic port sites (Steri-Strip care, showering after 48 hours, no water submersion for 2 weeks), drain site monitoring, return-to-ED precautions (fever >101°F, worsening pain, wound changes, inability to tolerate PO), activity restrictions (light activity 2 weeks, no heavy lifting >15 lbs for 4 weeks, desk work in 1 week, full construction duties pending surgeon clearance), and antibiotic completion instructions.

Pathology report on the appendectomy specimen confirming acute gangrenous appendicitis with transmural necrosis and perforation, acute serositis with fibrinopurulent exudate, no neoplasm identified, margins uninvolved.

diagnostic_reports.description: Only WBC (13,400/μL) and hemoglobin (13.8 g/dL) values are documented from this CBC. WBC trending down from admission value of 16,800. Insufficient components for a full report-with-results (missing hematocrit, platelets at minimum). Remaining CBC components presumably unremarkable.

Described as unremarkable with only creatinine (0.9 mg/dL) and glucose (102 mg/dL) specifically documented. Insufficient components for a full report-with-results (missing sodium, potassium, chloride, CO2, BUN, calcium at minimum).
Only WBC (11,200/μL) documented from this CBC draw, showing continued downtrend. Remaining components not reported.

Pathology report on appendectomy specimen. Findings: acute gangrenous appendicitis with transmural necrosis and perforation, acute serositis with fibrinopurulent exudate, no neoplasm identified, margins uninvolved. Findings discussed with patient by Dr. Nair on POD2.
